All States/Hawaii

Hawaii Dietitian License

Start with the only questions that matter: does your current license already cover Hawaii, what does endorsement cost, and how quickly can you get cleared to work?

Verified 2026-02-25 Board State Licensing Section - Dietitians Speed Mid
Fee $225 State transfer cost
Timeline 4 to 6 Weeks Current board estimate
Fingerprinting Required Background check
Temp License Not listed Faster start option
Reciprocity Steps Quick Facts Documents Board FAQ

How to Transfer Your License to Hawaii

No active interstate compact covers dietitian practice in Hawaii. Plan on a full endorsement application through State Licensing Section - Dietitians.

Good news: the exact cost, documentation, and board path are laid out below.

Before You Start

  1. Open the official board portal before gathering documents.
  2. Budget the endorsement fee and expected processing window.
  3. Assume you need full approval before starting work.
See Endorsement Steps Official Board Portal

Endorsement Steps

Work through these in order for the fastest path to board clearance in Hawaii.

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher. Complete an ACEND-approved supervised practice experience of not less than 900 hours.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

  3. Request Application Packet

    Contact the Hawaii DOH State Licensing Section directly at (808) 692-7400 to request an application packet, as it is not generally available online.

  4. Submit Application and Fee

    Return the completed application along with the $225 non-refundable fee and required verification documents by mail.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$225
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Renewal fee
Varies (Contact DOH)
Renewal cycle
Every 3 years (Must maintain active CDR)

Required Documents

  • Paper Application — Obtained by calling the DOH and completed accurately.
  • CDR Registration Card — A copy of your active Commission on Dietetic Registration card.
  • Official College Transcripts — Proof of the required accredited degree (foreign degrees require validation).

Board Contact

Board
State Licensing Section - Dietitians
Department
Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Care Assurance
Phone
(808) 692-7400
Email
doh.ohca@doh.hawaii.gov
Visit Official Board

Renewal

Renewal fee is Varies (Contact DOH). Renewal cycle: Every 3 years (Must maintain active CDR).

Frequently Asked Questions

The initial non-refundable fee for a Hawaii Dietitian License is $225.

Hawaii dietitian licenses are valid for three years from the date of issue and must be renewed triennially. You should contact the DOH to confirm current renewal fees.

No. You must contact the State Licensing Section directly at (808) 692-7400 to request the application packet.

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
